field_mismatches.arbitration_note: The filing says the Board authorized management to work with BofA Merrill Lynch. Alex codes this as an IB event; pipeline codes it as a target_sale. Both interpretations are defensible: it is the board authorizing exploration (target_sale) via their existing advisor (IB). The formal IB engagement letter was not until May 31.

The filing says BofA, as instructed by the Board, telephoned Party A. Party A then indicated interest. Alex codes it as Target Interest (target approached Party A); pipeline codes it as bidder_interest (Party A expressed interest). Both capture a different facet of the same event. The approach was target-initiated, so Alex's 'Target Interest' is slightly more precise about directionality.

The filing says '$17.00 to $19.00 per share'. This is a range, not a point estimate. Pipeline correctly codes it as range_low/range_high with no per_share. Alex codes bid_value=17 (the low end) with upper=19. The pipeline range representation is more accurate.

field_mismatches.pipeline: target_sale (board formed Special Committee, launched broader sale process)
field_mismatches.arbitration_note: The June 24 board meeting formed the Special Committee and launched the broader solicitation (contacting 50 parties). This is a target_sale launch, not a final round announcement. The final round announcement (requesting final indications by Sept 18) occurred on Sept 11. Alex appears to have mislabeled this as 'Final Round Inf Ann' when it is actually the formal sale process launch.

The filing says 'June 30, 2013, Party C entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement'. June 30 is the correct date. Alex's precise date of June 20 appears to be a typo; the rough date of June 30 is correct.

field_mismatches.pipeline: Formal (enrichment rule 2.5: after final round announcement)
field_mismatches.arbitration_note: Both agree this is Formal. No mismatch.

field_mismatches.pipeline: Informal (enrichment rule 1.0: indication of interest with range)
field_mismatches.arbitration_note: This is the Party A best-and-final offer submitted on the Sept 18 deadline. The filing says Party A 'reiterated ... that its previous indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share was its best and final offer.' Since it was submitted in response to the formal final round request by the Sept 18 deadline, Alex classifies it as Formal. Pipeline classified it Informal due to formality signals (indication_of_interest=true, contains_range=true). Alex is correct: this is a final-round response and should be Formal.

Both agree this is not an all-cash offer. The filing says '$19.00 of cash to be paid at closing and the remaining per share price to be paid in the form of options'. Pipeline codes consideration_type=mixed; Alex codes all_cash=0. Both correct.

field_mismatches.pipeline: Informal (enrichment rule 1.0: indication of interest signal)
field_mismatches.arbitration_note: Party B submitted this revised indication of interest on the Sept 18 final round deadline. The filing says 'Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest'. Although the filing uses 'indication of interest' language (which triggered the pipeline's Informal classification), this was submitted in response to the formal final round request and should be classified Formal. Alex is correct.

field_mismatches.pipeline: drop (no subtype; Party C did not submit a revised proposal by deadline)
field_mismatches.arbitration_note: Both agree. Alex labels this as 'Drop', pipeline labels it 'drop'. Party C failed to respond to the Sept 18 deadline. Equivalent.

field_mismatches.note: Different granularity. Board authorized exploration with existing advisor; not yet a formal engagement letter.
Directional difference. Target initiated the call (Alex's view); bidder expressed interest on the call (pipeline's view). Both are defensible.
Pipeline correctly uses range. Alex uses bid_value=lower_end, which is a convention, not an error.

June 24 was the sale process launch (Special Committee formation + solicitation of 50 parties), not a final round announcement. The final round announcement was Sept 11 (evt_016). Alex may have confused the sale launch with the first round request.
Alex's precise date of June 20 is likely a typo. Filing says June 30.

Party A's Sept 18 best-and-final was a final round response. Pipeline's enrichment rule 1.0 (indication_of_interest signal) overrode rule 2.5 (after_final_round_announcement). This is a pipeline enrichment bug: the after_final_round_announcement flag is true, but the IOI language triggered the informal classification. Fix: final round responses should be Formal regardless of IOI language.
Same bug as evt_022. Party B's Sept 18 final round response was classified Informal due to indication_of_interest signal, but should be Formal as a final-round deadline response.

pipeline_only.summary: Other unnamed financial bidders NDA (aggregate, undated)
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline correctly captures the aggregate 18 financial bidders NDA event. This corresponds to Alex's aggregate row BidderID=2. Matched at the aggregate level, not as an atomic orphan.

The filing clearly states on June 28 that Pamplona expressed interest in acquiring Mac-Gray via CSC. Alex does not have a separate bidder_interest row for CSC/Pamplona; Alex's BidderID=0.9 row covers the CSC/Pamplona interest date but is labeled 'Bidder Interest' for 'CSC/Pamplona' with date 2013-06-28 -- wait, actually checking the CSV: row 0.9 is 'Bidder Interest, CSC/Pamplona, S, 2013-06-28'. This matches. See matched pair update below.

The filing says on September 11 the Special Committee instructed BofA to request final indications by Sept 18. Alex has BidderID=16 'Final Round Ann' on 2013-09-11, which matches. See matched pair update below.

pipeline_only_corrected.note: After reviewing the CSV more carefully, evt_007 (CSC/Pamplona bidder_interest 2013-06-28) matches CSV row 0.9 'Bidder Interest, CSC/Pamplona' and Alex has no separate row. evt_016 (final_round_inf_ann 2013-09-11) matches Alex BidderID=16 'Final Round Ann'. These are actually matched pairs, moved below.

additional_matched_pairs.note: The CSV row matches. Alex does not have a corresponding row for this event specifically (Alex's BidderID=5 is the CSC/Pamplona NDA on 2013-07-11). This event has no Alex counterpart.

field_mismatches.arbitration_note: The filing says the Sept 11 instruction was to 'request final indications of interest' -- these are informal indications, not binding formal bids. The term 'indications of interest' implies an informal round. Pipeline's 'final_round_inf_ann' is more precise than Alex's 'Final Round Ann'.

The filing says 'on October 14, 2013, the merger agreement was executed'. The execution date is Oct 14, not Sept 21. Alex's precise date of Sept 21 (the date of the CSC/Pamplona final proposal) appears to be a coding error; the rough date of Oct 14 is correct.

alex_only.note: The filing says on May 14, 2013, the Transaction Committee determined to terminate BofA Merrill Lynch's engagement letter entered into for the 2011-2012 Discussions. This is a real event (termination of the prior engagement before re-engaging on May 31). Pipeline did not extract this because the pipeline starts the deal narrative at the April 5 board meeting and treats the May 31 engagement as the single IB event. Alex's date of May 15 is close to the filing's May 14.

Alex coded Party A's June 21 unsolicited proposal as a separate 'Bidder Sale' event in addition to the proposal row (BidderID=1). The filing says Party A 'submitted an unsolicited proposal'. Alex uses 'Bidder Sale' to mark that Party A initiated a bid. Pipeline captures this in evt_004 (proposal) which includes the unsolicited nature in the summary. This is an Alex coding convention (separate process row + proposal row for the same event), not a missing pipeline event.

Alex marks a 'Final Round Inf' (informal round deadline) on July 23, which is the date indications of interest were due. The filing says parties were 'instructed to submit by July 23, 2013.' Pipeline does not have a separate first-round-deadline event. This is a valid process milestone that the pipeline omitted.

Alex marks a 'Final Round Inf Ext Ann' (informal round extension announcement) on July 25. The filing discusses the Special Committee's July 25 meeting where they decided on staged disclosure and to arrange management meetings (effectively extending the process). This is a valid process milestone.

Alex marks a 'Final Round Inf Ext' (informal round extension deadline) around Sept 9, when revised indications were submitted before the formal Sept 18 deadline. This is a process milestone marking the continuation of the informal round.

Alex has a separate 'Final Round' row with announcement date Sept 11 and deadline Sept 18. Pipeline captures this in evt_016 (final_round_inf_ann). This is an Alex convention of having both announcement and deadline as separate rows.

On September 24, Mac-Gray entered into an exclusivity agreement with CSC/Pamplona. This effectively dropped Party A from the process. The filing does not describe an explicit notification to Party A, but entering exclusivity with CSC/Pamplona on Sept 24 implicitly excludes Party A. Pipeline does not have an explicit drop event for Party A.
Same as Party A above. Entering exclusivity with CSC/Pamplona on Sept 24 implicitly dropped Party B. Pipeline does not have an explicit drop event for Party B.

aggregate_rows.pipeline_summary: Other unnamed financial bidders NDA (group_size=16 in actors, total 18 financial bidders in filing)
aggregate_rows.note: Alex says '16 financial bidders' signed NDAs. The filing says 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C). Pipeline captures this as a grouped actor with group_size=16 (18 minus the 2 named ones). Alex's count of 16 matches the pipeline's unnamed count. Both exclude Party B and Party C from this aggregate. The date differs: Alex uses 2013-07-15 (rough midpoint); pipeline has no date (spans 'over the next two months').

Alex records the 16 unnamed financial bidders dropping out by July 25 (the deadline for initial indications of interest). The filing does not explicitly describe these parties dropping, but only Party B, Party C, and CSC/Pamplona submitted indications of interest by the deadline. Pipeline does not capture an explicit drop event for the unnamed financial bidders. This is an inference Alex makes from the timeline.

Pipeline evt_027 (sale_press_release 2013-10-15) has no direct Alex row match. The pipeline CSV has this as the final row. Alex's last row is BidderID=24 (Executed). Alex does not have a sale_press_release row.

notes: 23 of 27 pipeline events have Alex counterparts. The remaining 4 pipeline events (evt_006 aggregate NDA, evt_007 CSC/Pamplona bidder_interest, evt_027 sale_press_release, and one process event) are filing-grounded.

7 Alex-only rows exist: 1 IB Terminated (valid, pipeline omitted prior engagement termination), 1 Bidder Sale (Alex coding convention), 4 process/round milestone rows (Final Round Inf, Final Round Inf Ext Ann, Final Round Inf Ext, Final Round), and 2 DropTarget rows for Party A and Party B (implied by exclusivity, not explicitly stated in filing). All are likely_grounded.
3 aggregate Alex rows: 16 financial bidders NDA (soft match), 16 financial bidders Drop (likely grounded inference), sale_press_release (pipeline only).

Material disagreements: (1) Alex BidderID=1.5 labels June 24 as 'Final Round Inf Ann' -- filing supports pipeline's 'target_sale' (sale launch). (2) Pipeline classifies Sept 18 Party A (evt_022) and Party B (evt_023) proposals as Informal; Alex says Formal. Filing supports Alex: these were final-round-deadline responses. This is a pipeline enrichment rule priority bug where indication-of-interest language overrides the after_final_round_announcement flag.

Minor disagreements: bid_note taxonomy differences (IB vs target_sale, bidder_interest vs Target Interest), Alex typo on Party C NDA date (June 20 vs June 30), Alex typo on Executed precise date (Sept 21 vs Oct 14).
action_items: Fix enrichment bid_type classification: final-round-deadline responses should be classified Formal even when filing uses indication-of-interest language. Affects evt_022 (Party A Sept 18) and evt_023 (Party B Sept 18).
Consider adding DropTarget events for Party A and Party B when exclusivity is granted to another bidder.
Consider extracting the May 14 IB termination event (termination of the 2011-2012 Discussions engagement).
Consider adding process milestone events for round deadlines (July 23 first-round deadline, July 25 staged-disclosure extension).
